rulings.Case Title: CALVIN SMITHSON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Defendant Supro Corporation For Order Allowing Service Of Pared Down List Of Expert Witnesses Within 30 Days Of Trial Pursuant To Ccp 473(B)
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Supro Corporation's motion for an order allowing service of a pared down expert list within 30 days of trial is granted. Supro has shown that its failure to timely serve the list occurred as a result of excusable neglect and plaintiffs have failed to establish any prejudice. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: IN RE: PETITION OF GERALDINE HILT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Amended Petition Of Geraldine Hilt To Perpetuate Testiony Of Robert Hilt By Videotaped Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Petitioner's motion to take the deposition of Robert Hilt is granted. Dana Companies' request to have the motion heard by Judge McBride is denied. All pretrial motions in all asbestos cases and petitions, excluding only those expressly reserved for another department, are now heard in department 220. The fact that Judge McBride presided over the truncated trial of the personal injury action does not require or support his hearing this motion. The personal injury case will be placed on department 206's order to show cause why the case has not been dismissed calendar for April 29, 2010. Petitioner has shown good cause for the deposition. The deposition taken by defendants of Mr. Hilt in the personal injury case is not a substitute for a perpetuation of testimony deposition. Dana has not shown that it has been prejudiced by not being included in the initial petition. = (220/HEK)
